description: A designed vertical infographic / statistics card branded by The White House. The top half is a photo of President Donald Trump speaking at a podium microphone, wearing a dark navy suit, white shirt, and pink/red tie with a flag lapel pin, against a deep blue stage backdrop with a vignette. Overlaid in large white serif/sans display text is the title 'Real Wage GAINS' ('Real Wage' italic serif, 'GAINS' bold). The bottom half is a white chart panel titled 'Biden Real Wage Losses & Trump Real Wage Gains for Typical Worker', with a legend distinguishing 'Biden Decline in Real Wages (Total)' (navy) from 'Trump Real Wage Increases (1-Year, Annualized)' (red). A bar chart plots 'Real wage change ($)': a single navy bar showing 'All Pvt. Sector' at -$2,919 (labeled 'BIDEN LOSSES') versus four red bars labeled 'TRUMP GAINS' — All Pvt. Sector $1,048, Factory Worker $1,300, Construction $1,826, and Mining $3,336. A dark footer bar reads 'THE WHITE HOUSE / WASHINGTON', with the presidential seal centered at the very bottom. The design technique is a contrast/comparison infographic that frames the prior administration as causing wage losses and the current one as delivering gains. Target audience: general public, framing economic data favorably for the administration.
notable_text: Real Wage GAINS; Biden Real Wage Losses & Trump Real Wage Gains for Typical Worker; BIDEN LOSSES -$2,919; TRUMP GAINS $1,048 / $1,300 / $1,826 / $3,336; THE WHITE HOUSE WASHINGTON
